content.full_text:
.                    .




                                      California
                                      Fair Political
                                      Practices Commission




                                                    September 24, 1987


Helen S. Kawagoe, CMC
City Clerk
P. O. Box 6234
Carson, CA  90749
                                                    Re: Your Request for Advice
                                                        Our File No. A-87-240

Dear Ms. Kawagoe:

       This is to confirm my telephone advice to you regarding the
flyer you sent to me entitled "The Good, The Bad And The Ugly
News."  The flyer has been circulated by former candidate Aaron
Carter and a group called Concerned Homeowners of Carson.  The flyer
discusses a zoning matter pending before the Planning Commission and
urges residents to sign petitions and attend Planning Commission
meetings.  You have asked whether you should notify Mr. Carter and
the Concerned Homeowners of Carson of any campaign disclosure filing
obligations under the Political Reform Act.1/


       I advised you that Mr. Carter and the Concerned Homeowners of
Carson have not incurred a filing obligation in connection with the
flyer for the following reasons:

       1) Mr. Carter is not currently a "candidate" as defined in
Government Code Section 82007 and, therefore, is not required to
disclose payments made or received in connection with disseminating
the flyer.

       2) Mr. Carter and the Concerned Homeowners of Carson are not a
"committee" as defined in Section 82013 because payments made or
received in connection with disseminating this type of flyer are not
"contributions" or "expenditures" as defined in Sections 82015 and
82025 and Regulations 18215 and 18225.  This is because the flyer
does not expressly advocate the election or defeat of candidates or
measures.  (Section 82031, Regulation 18225(b).)


_____________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.




428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-

Helen S. Kawagoe
September 24, 1987
Page 2


Please let me know if I can be of further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Carla J. Wardlow
                                        Political Reform Consultant

sections.question: Whether you should notify Mr. Carter and the Concerned Homeowners of Carson of any campaign disclosure filing obligations under the Political Reform Act in connection with a flyer entitled 'The Good, The Bad And The Ugly News' that discusses a zoning matter pending before the Planning Commission and urges residents to sign petitions and attend Planning Commission meetings.
sections.conclusion: Mr. Carter and the Concerned Homeowners of Carson have not incurred a filing obligation in connection with the flyer because: (1) Mr. Carter is not currently a 'candidate' as defined in Government Code section 82007 and therefore is not required to disclose payments; and (2) Mr. Carter and the Concerned Homeowners of Carson are not a 'committee' as defined in section 82013 because payments made or received in connection with disseminating this type of flyer are not 'contributions' or 'expenditures' as defined in Sections 82015 and 82025. The flyer does not expressly advocate the election or defeat of candidates or measures.
sections.question_synthetic: Must former candidate Aaron Carter and the group Concerned Homeowners of Carson comply with campaign disclosure filing obligations under the Political Reform Act for distributing a flyer about a pending zoning matter that encourages public participation in Planning Commission proceedings?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No campaign disclosure filing obligation exists because the flyer does not expressly advocate for or against the election or defeat of any candidates or measures. Therefore, neither Mr. Carter nor Concerned Homeowners of Carson qualify as a 'candidate' or 'committee' under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear advice letter with telephone advice confirmation. Question and conclusion sections identified from letter body. Document dated September 24, 1987, File No. A-87-240. Signed by Carla J. Herdlow, Political Reform Consultant.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether you should notify Mr. Carter and the Concerned Homeowners of Carson of any campaign disclosure filing obligations under the Political Reform Act in connection with a flyer entitled 'The Good, The Bad And The Ugly News' that discusses a zoning matter pending before the Planning Commission and urges residents to sign petitions and attend Planning Commission meetings.

CONCLUSION: Mr. Carter and the Concerned Homeowners of Carson have not incurred a filing obligation in connection with the flyer because: (1) Mr. Carter is not currently a 'candidate' as defined in Government Code section 82007 and therefore is not required to disclose payments; and (2) Mr. Carter and the Concerned Homeowners of Carson are not a 'committee' as defined in section 82013 because payments made or received in connection with disseminating this type of flyer are not 'contributions' or 'expenditures' as defined in Sections 82015 and 82025. The flyer does not expressly advocate the election or defeat of candidates or measures.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises City Clerk Helen Kawagoe that former candidate Aaron Carter and Concerned Homeowners of Carson have no campaign disclosure filing obligations for distributing a flyer about a pending zoning matter because the flyer does not expressly advocate for election or defeat of candidates or measures.
